test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 1200.001s.
KubeadmControlPlane object k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-3niv1k/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k-control-plane was not initialized in time
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:
KubeadmControlPlane is not yet initialized
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1200.001s.
KubeadmControlPlane object k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-3niv1k/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k-control-plane was not initialized in time
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:
KubeadmControlPlane is not yet initialized
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:266 @ 06/09/26 21:39:19.528

ai_summary.summary: Control plane VM failed to initialize due to Azure zonal capacity allocation failure

ai_analysis.root_cause: The KubeadmControlPlane failed to initialize because the first control plane machine's Azure VM provisioning failed with a 'ZonalAllocationFailed' error. From the CAPZ controller logs at artifacts/clusters/bootstrap/logs/capz-system/capz-controller-manager/capz-controller-manager-d6c54db8f-md775/manager.log, we can see repeated errors like 'ERROR CODE: ZonalAllocationFailed' with the message 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone.' This prevented the AzureMachine from becoming ready, which in turn blocked the KubeadmControlPlane from initializing. The AzureMachine status shows 'vmState: Failed' and while the 'VMRunning' condition is True, the overall machine is not ready for bootstrap. The CAPI controller logs also show it was waiting for the infrastructure provider to set status.ready on the AzureMachine.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Modify the cluster template to specify a different Azure region or availability zone that has sufficient capacity for the requested VM size (Standard_B2s). Alternatively, consider using a different VM size that is more readily available in the current zone. The template can be found in artifacts/clusters/bootstrap/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k-cluster-template.yaml.

test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 1200.000s.
Timed out waiting for 3 control plane machines to exist
Expected
    <int>: 2
to equal
    <int>: 3
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1200.000s.
Timed out waiting for 3 control plane machines to exist
Expected
    <int>: 2
to equal
    <int>: 3
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/pkg/mod/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test@v1.13.2/framework/controlplane_helpers.go:115 @ 06/09/26 21:44:37.181

ai_summary.summary: Control plane machine failed to provision due to Azure zonal capacity constraints

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test failure occurred because the third control plane machine (k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-cirl41-control-plane-779jf) failed to provision in Azure due to 'ZonalAllocationFailed' errors. From the CAPZ controller logs, we can see repeated attempts to create the VM resource capz-e2e-d3buei/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-cirl41-control-plane-779jf failed with the error message: 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone.' This is a transient infrastructure issue where Azure cannot allocate the requested VM size in the specified zone. The first two control plane machines (hk8qj and hx49l) successfully provisioned and completed their cloud-init processes, but the third machine never became available, causing the test to timeout waiting for 3 control plane machines to exist. The kubelet logs for the successfully provisioned machines show normal operation, while the third machine's logs are empty because the VM was never created.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is due to temporary Azure capacity constraints in a specific zone. The evidence shows this is a transient infrastructure issue (ZonalAllocationFailed) that would resolve by either retrying the test (allowing Azure to potentially allocate in a different zone or at a different time when capacity is available) or modifying the cluster template to specify a different VM size or zone. The specific artifacts consulted were the CAPZ controller manager logs showing the ZonalAllocationFailed errors and the machine logs showing successful operation of the first two machines.
